title: run: check if SELinux is enabled
body: When we're built with support for SELinux, refrain from setting process and mount labels if SELinux isn't detected as enabled at runtime.

comments.body: Are you seeing an issue with this?

I think we had a report from IRC where the user had created the container while SELinux was enabled, but hit an error while attempting to run it after disabling SELinux.  It's probably not a 100% fix, but I think it's an improvement.

Change to selinux.GetEnabled() and then we can merge.

EnforceMode, reads /etc/selinux/config and tells you what is set there,  Has nothing to do with host until the next reboot.
@rhatdan are you sure?  The godoc for `EnforceMode()` doesn't mention that, and a casual reading doesn't suggest it.

```
man selinux_getenforcemode
selinux_getenforcemode(3)                                                                               SELinux API documentation                                                                              selinux_getenforcemode(3)

NAME
       selinux_getenforcemode - get the enforcing state of SELinux

SYNOPSIS
       #include <selinux/selinux.h>

       int selinux_getenforcemode(int *enforce);

DESCRIPTION
       selinux_getenforcemode() Reads the contents of the /etc/selinux/config file to determine how the system was setup to run SELinux.

       Sets the value of enforce to 1 if SELinux should be run in enforcing mode.  Sets the value of enforce to 0 if SELinux should be run in permissive mode.  Sets the value of enforce to -1 if SELinux should be disabled.

RETURN VALUE
       On success, zero is returned.  On failure, -1 is returned.

SEE ALSO
       selinux(8)

dwalsh@redhat.com                                                                                              25 May 2004                                                                                     selinux_getenforcemode(3)
```

Sadly the golang does not match.

But the interface we want to expose is GetEnabled()
